item.headline: Member of Technical Staff at Thinking Machines Lab | Post-training Team | Large-scale RL | Multi-agent Systems | Real-time Multi-modal Models
item.about: I'm a second-year PhD student in EECS at UC Berkeley. I obtained my B.A. in Computer Science at UC Berkeley. I am primarily interested in multi-modal generative models as well as efficient deep learning.

multiLocaleHeadline.headline: Member of Technical Staff at Thinking Machines Lab | Post-training Team | Large-scale RL | Multi-agent Systems | Real-time Multi-modal Models

experience.description: Working with post-training team on large-scale reinforcement learning, multi-agent systems, and real-time multi-modal models.
Working as a Research Scientist Intern at GenAI Llama Pretraining Team

•	Working with Prof. Trevor Darrell and Prof. Adam Yala on multi-modal generative models as well as efficient deep learning with medical imaging applications
•	Previously worked with Prof. Stella Yu on unsupervised video segmentation, long-tailed recognition, unsupervised representation learning, and semi-supervised learning

Helped student groups at Berkeley with website maintenance on Linux servers in staff hours
Developed vivhost, a virtual host configuration validator in Python
Helped test and optimize the Network File System on Linux servers
Working on multi-modal research at NVIDIA Research (Deep Imagination Research team)

experience.position: Research Intern at Berkeley Institute for Data Science
experience.description:
- Worked with David Anthroff to develop Queryverse.jl, the core data science software stack for the Julia language
- Wrote extensions for libraries such as NamedTuple and FileIO in Julia

experience.companyName: Berkeley Chinese Students and Scholars Association
experience.description: •	Doing maintenance for the group website (Linux Administration, PHP)

•	Developed Verified License for Professionals, which extends existing license verification service to professionals such as doctors and lawyers with React, Node.js, NoSQL, and Python (Pyramid)
•	Developing enhanced purchase flow for license verification service which intends to improve the license verification rate from 50% to 80%, which is expected to bring Yelp about $2M per year
•	Improved internal short URL service so that it gives suggestions when a nonexistent short URL is typed at a corporate hackathon

Designed a hybrid mobile app for the organization with 200, 000+ users (Angular, TypeScript, and Laravel)
Developed a student event-sharing platform with 100, 000+ users (Vue.js and Laravel)
Built several web apps for events such as 2018 New Year Offer Generator (generate offer images with Canvas) and Match Your School (match students with suitable schools, with Vue.js and Node.js)
Migrated the business logic layer of the city bus e-payment system to a C framework (previously in Pro*C)

Developed freight evaluation system which is embedded into their online store now
Wrote the shipping part of the web back-end for the online store with DHL APIs in PHP
Designed and taught the Advanced Placement Computer Science A course (Java)

projects.title: Code Recipe, an online interactive textbook platform
projects.description:
•	Designed a website to host textbooks with interactive features such as embedded coding area in the text (JavaScript, HTML, SCSS, Node.js, Vue, Webpack, Redis, PHP, and Laravel)
•	Provide free online resources for students to learn Java, Python, and Graphical Programming

projects.title: Personal Website
projects.description: A personal blog built on Jekyll about my personal thoughts and interests.

projects.title: Panopath Student-Matching Platform
projects.associatedWith: Associated with Panopath Education and Technology
projects.description: This is a hybrid mobile app for iOS and Android devices which acts as a forum for colleges and matches high school students with college students so that they can ask them about experiences in college. This platform now has more than 200, 000 users.

projects.title: Pano Activity WeChat Mini Program
projects.associatedWith: Associated with Panopath Education and Technology
projects.description: This is a student event-sharing platform with 100, 000+ users with a Vue-like development stack called WePY as front-end and Laravel as back-end.

projects.title: Minu, a menu translator app
projects.description:
This is an iOS app that uses OpenCV to recognize text in the image and to display dish description in multiple languages. It was programmed with Objective-C, Swift, and PHP (backend) as a personal project.

An article, An English-to-Chinese Menu Scanning and Translating Application Based on Mobile Phone Camera, was published in journal Electronics World about the app.

volunteering.description:
Mentored hackers for three days with Python, JavaScript, and Linux in the world's largest collegiate hackathon
Helped teams solved problems about CORS, Ajax, and React.js
Helped with habitat restoration, pulled out weeds, and watered grass in Palo Alto area
